jokes.text: We found that if someone is actually on nbc, They're 4% more likely to watch it.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining the promotional value of the contest
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and low percentage increase
jokes.explanation: Perfect corporate-speak satire with an absurdly precise statistic that feels both realistic and ridiculous.

jokes.text: [robotic voice] keep the white house white.
jokes.setup: Previous year's contest winner doing something inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Racist robotic voice statement on live TV
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about live TV disasters and inappropriate contestants. Effective cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Oh, really? 'cause this one is typed on the inside Of a bloody halter top.
jokes.setup: Jack saying reading contest essays won't kill her
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely specific description of one essay's medium
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and disturbing visual that escalates the 'weirdos' theme perfectly. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I thought you said a man should never wear pastel Unless he's a black guy on easter.
jokes.setup: Jack asking about tie preferences
jokes.punchline: Recalling Jack's very specific fashion rule with racial component
jokes.explanation: Great character consistency joke that feels like a real Jack Donaghy fashion rule. Specific enough to be funny.

jokes.text: I need to tone down my natural aura Of strength and sexual dominance.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why he needs to wear pastel
jokes.punchline: The absurdly grandiose description of his 'problem'
jokes.explanation: Peak Jack Donaghy character comedy - ridiculously self-aggrandizing while addressing a real PR concern.

jokes.text: as it turns out, amnesty international Is nothing but a company that makes and sells candles.
jokes.setup: Jack listing useless organizations in finding Avery
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong understanding of what Amnesty International is
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist misunderstanding that feels both stupid and plausible for Jack's worldview.

jokes.text: That explains all the vigils. That is a good scam.
jokes.setup: Jack's misunderstanding of Amnesty International
jokes.punchline: Liz playing along with the candle company logic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurd premise, with Liz's deadpan acceptance making it funnier.

jokes.text: Media is a plural noun. Put this tie on.
jokes.setup: Jack saying 'the media have moved on'
jokes.punchline: Liz's pedantic grammar correction followed by abrupt subject change
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment - pedantic correction followed by practical action. Great economy.

jokes.text: It's called... Rhythm and news.
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning Matt Lauer's band
jokes.punchline: The terrible pun band name combining news and rhythm & blues
jokes.explanation: Solid pun that feels like something a morning show host would actually do. The pause builds it nicely.

jokes.text: I know I'm a big shot now with my suits And a desk lamp that I control
jokes.setup: Kenneth talking about his new job success
jokes.punchline: The absurdly minor 'perk' of controlling a desk lamp
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth character comedy - his innocent view of what constitutes power and success.

jokes.text: On behalf of the mother[bleep] Standards department, I, gaylord felcher, present you With the 'employee of the [bleep] week' award.
jokes.setup: Kenneth receiving an award from Standards department
jokes.punchline: The profanity-laden speech from the guy who's supposed to prevent profanity
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor - the standards department being completely non-standard. Name 'Gaylord Felcher' adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Can you? You just did.
jokes.setup: Hazel asking 'can I speak with you?'
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's literal interpretation of the question
jokes.explanation: Classic Kenneth literal-mindedness. Simple but effective character comedy.

jokes.text: My parents were technically brothers--
jokes.setup: Hazel asking what Kenneth's problem is
jokes.punchline: Kenneth revealing disturbing family history as if it's minor
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect Kenneth innocence. Great character-specific joke about his background.

jokes.text: Just like nana parcell, my heartworm.
jokes.setup: Kenneth saying Tracy has a special place in his heart
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to his grandmother, who is apparently a parasite in his heart
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Kenneth biology joke. The casual delivery of something so disturbing is perfect.

jokes.text: There was a mine collapse in china. Turns out, to keep labor costs down, All the miners there are babies. They pay 'em in yogurt.
jokes.setup: Pete explaining why Jack's segment was bumped for real news
jokes.punchline: The increasingly absurd details about baby miners paid in yogurt
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdism. Each detail gets more ridiculous but maintains internal logic.

jokes.text: Remember when we used to be the world leader in baby cuteness? I can't compete with that.
jokes.setup: Learning about Chinese baby miners
jokes.punchline: Jack's competitive corporate response to baby exploitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack response - turning human tragedy into corporate competition. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: adult dogs can't fit in the baby mines, So they're lowering down puppies to bring in supplies.
jokes.setup: Already established baby mine scenario
jokes.punchline: Logical extension with puppies as supply dogs
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that follows the internal logic of the absurd premise perfectly.

jokes.text: do not write another sketch about krang From teenage mutant ninja turtles. No one knows who krang is. It would be a waste of time to talk about krang on television. No more krang!
jokes.setup: Liz giving writing instructions
jokes.punchline: Increasingly emphatic denial while repeatedly mentioning the supposedly unknown character
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about obscure references, with escalating repetition making it funnier.

jokes.text: Krang!
jokes.setup: Just finished saying no one knows who Krang is
jokes.punchline: Immediately shouting Krang when startled
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback with great timing. Shows that Liz clearly does know Krang.

jokes.text: 'cause I took one of those 'which gossip girl are you?' quizzes, And it said I was the dad's guitar.
jokes.setup: Hazel asking if Liz is sure about giving advice woman to woman
jokes.punchline: The impossibly specific and nonsensical quiz result
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and specific. Perfect Liz self-deprecation with an impossibly weird result.

jokes.text: Your rack is like, 'pow!'
jokes.setup: Hazel complimenting Liz
jokes.punchline: The awkward, overly familiar way of complimenting someone's breasts
jokes.explanation: Good character work for Hazel's inappropriate oversharing tendency.

jokes.text: And then he had his way with me. With his eyes.
jokes.setup: Hazel describing Kenneth's inappropriate behavior
jokes.punchline: The clarification that it was only visual, making it less serious but funnier
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure that makes Kenneth's 'assault' both innocent and creepy.

jokes.text: They're all a bunch of gays.
jokes.setup: Liz saying 'the male gaze'
jokes.punchline: Hazel mishearing it as 'male gays'
jokes.explanation: Good character-based misunderstanding showing Hazel's lack of sophistication.

jokes.text: Leslie or courtney?
jokes.setup: Liz asking what people call ambitious women
jokes.punchline: Hazel giving actual names instead of understanding it's about labels
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Hazel's literal-mindedness and lack of feminist awareness.

jokes.text: Just like the karate kid-- hilary swankarate kid iv.
jokes.setup: Liz giving mentorship advice
jokes.punchline: Confusing Karate Kid with Million Dollar Baby through awkward portmanteau
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture confusion joke, though the construction is a bit awkward.

jokes.text: Either cnn gets back on the avery jessup story, Or I'll tell everyone your real name, steel hammerhands.
jokes.setup: Jack threatening someone on the phone
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous fake name for Wolf Blitzer
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist name that sounds like a cartoon character. Perfect 30 Rock style.

jokes.text: I want you to hire a giant to stand in the background Of every scene I'm in to make me look daintier.
jokes.setup: Jenna making an actor request
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous and vain nature of wanting a giant for comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna vanity request. Absurd but feels totally in character.

jokes.text: It's why lamar odom married that giant kardashian girl.
jokes.setup: Explaining why the giant strategy works
jokes.punchline: The backwards logic about the Odom-Kardashian marriage
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference that fits Jenna's twisted logic about relationships.

jokes.text: 'I'm home, jack. And I brought back a bunch of oriental sex powders.'
jokes.setup: Jack fantasizing about Avery returning
jokes.punchline: The specific and inappropriate thing he imagines her saying
jokes.explanation: Good character insight into Jack's fantasies and his relationship dynamic with Avery.

jokes.text: I played italian party whore in the amanda knox story.
jokes.setup: Jenna saying she knows about TV movies
jokes.punchline: Her typically inappropriate and minor role in a serious story
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna character moment - finding a way to make a tragedy about her.

jokes.text: Send a bunch of pies to the oxygen network.
jokes.setup: Jack saying they need to slow down Oxygen's competing movie
jokes.punchline: The absurdly simple sabotage plan
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd corporate sabotage that's both innocent and ridiculous.

jokes.text: tracy's jaw is tired from pretending to be pac-man.
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking about Tracy's food preparation
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous reason for his jaw fatigue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy Jordan absurdity delivered matter-of-factly by Hazel.

jokes.text: I definitely have a penis.
jokes.setup: Hazel accusing him of acting superior because he has a penis
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's matter-of-fact confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Kenneth's literal honesty about anatomy.

jokes.text: You wanna be bossed around by this trailer park hitler?
jokes.setup: Hazel competing with Kenneth for Tracy's loyalty
jokes.punchline: Calling Kenneth 'trailer park hitler'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation that's both offensive and character-appropriate for Hazel.

jokes.text: are you busy regretting your life choices, For example, missing your window for having children?
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking if Liz has a second
jokes.punchline: His brutal assessment of her life situation
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's innocent cruelty delivered as casual observation

jokes.text: I bet even hillary clinton has to put up with this crap From whoever was secretary of state before her.
jokes.setup: Liz defending Hazel against Kenneth's sexism
jokes.punchline: Not knowing that Condoleezza Rice was Secretary of State before Clinton
jokes.explanation: Good political ignorance joke that fits Liz's character blind spots.

jokes.text: 'leave girl alone or you deal with me, surf shop owner.'
jokes.setup: Liz quoting Mr. Miyagi to Kenneth
jokes.punchline: The wrong quote that includes an irrelevant detail
jokes.explanation: Decent callback that continues Liz's confused movie references.

jokes.text: Put a bag of popcorn in the microwave beforehand. That way, when you're done, you have a treat.
jokes.setup: Giving Hazel bedroom advice
jokes.punchline: The sad, practical, unromantic tip
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon character moment - practical to the point of being depressing about romance.

jokes.text: And you know, I used to weigh 800 pounds.
jokes.setup: Hazel describing the fat woman Razmig was with
jokes.punchline: The impossible weight that establishes her perspective
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation that's both absurd and explains Hazel's perspective.

jokes.text: Like by greeting him at the front door Wearing nothing but false teeth.
jokes.setup: Hazel asking how to spice up her relationship
jokes.punchline: The disturbing and nonsensical suggestion
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly disturbing and specific Hazel logic about romance.

jokes.text: If this were two years ago, I would sit on you till you died.
jokes.setup: Hazel being disappointed in Liz's mentorship
jokes.punchline: Reference to her previous weight and implied murder method
jokes.explanation: Great callback that escalates the weight joke into dark murder threat.

jokes.text: You mean lois lane's love affair with journalism?
jokes.setup: Jack asking about writing the greatest love story ever told
jokes.punchline: Liz's nerdy, work-focused interpretation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment - turning romance into work metaphor.

jokes.text: I said it.
jokes.setup: Just made the Lois Lane journalism joke
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging she just said something clever
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment where Liz is pleased with her own wordplay.

jokes.text: I guess I won't be able to go to the gym this weekend.
jokes.setup: Jack asking her to write a script by Monday
jokes.punchline: Pretending this will interfere with gym plans she obviously doesn't have
jokes.explanation: Good character-based sarcasm about Liz's non-existent fitness routine.

jokes.text: Don't worry, I'll bring you back a t-shirt from successtown. Ow! My groin!
jokes.setup: Liz rejecting Jack's advice confidently
jokes.punchline: Immediately hurting herself while trying to leave dramatically
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz physical comedy that undercuts her confidence

jokes.text: You were wrong. They do still make crossbows.
jokes.setup: Tracy returning to Kenneth
jokes.punchline: His focus on proving Kenneth wrong about crossbow availability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - focusing on the wrong details

jokes.text: We on a show within a show! My real name is tracy morgan!
jokes.setup: Tracy being sleep deprived and going crazy
jokes.punchline: Breaking the fourth wall about being on 30 Rock
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke that acknowledges the show's reality in a character-appropriate way.

jokes.text: No, kenneth, I let him buy a motorcycle ramp And no motorcycle!
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking if Hazel let Tracy buy a motorcycle
jokes.punchline: Her enabling the more dangerous half of the equation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly backwards logic that's somehow more dangerous

jokes.text: We on a show within a show! My real name is tracy morgan!
jokes.setup: Tracy going crazy from lack of sleep
jokes.punchline: Breaking the fourth wall about the show's structure
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock meta-humor delivered through Tracy's breakdown

jokes.text: Those are my toes he's sucking on. And he did not want to.
jokes.setup: Hazel showing Kenneth the Page Six coverage
jokes.punchline: The disturbing detail about forced toe-sucking
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor that shows how far Hazel will go for attention.

jokes.text: I own a magnet that can scramble her pacemaker.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining her friendship with Donna from casting
jokes.punchline: Revealing she has a weapon against Donna's medical device
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna sociopathy delivered as casual friendship detail

jokes.text: just because my college Got tipped over by those miami heat fans.
jokes.setup: Jack implying Jenna didn't go to a good school
jokes.punchline: Her school being literally destroyed by sports fans
jokes.explanation: Absurd backstory that explains Jenna's educational background

jokes.text: Practical, emotional, and there's actual intelligence, Which is what I'm talking about.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining different types of intelligence
jokes.punchline: His brutal assessment that Jenna has none of the real kind
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack condescension delivered as helpful explanation

jokes.text: Would a dumb person be able to think of something amazing To say as she leaves?
jokes.setup: Jack saying Jenna lacks intelligence for the Avery role
jokes.punchline: Jenna's attempt to prove intelligence by... not proving intelligence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna logic - attempting to prove intelligence while completely failing to do so.

jokes.text: what? Who is this, hah-zel? I no permit you talk other woman.
jokes.setup: Razmig appearing and seeing Hazel talk to Liz
jokes.punchline: The controlling, broken English response
jokes.explanation: Basic stereotypical controlling boyfriend character. Does the job but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: You have pictures? Fat nudes?
jokes.setup: Liz calling Hazel a big woman
jokes.punchline: Razmig's inappropriate immediate interest in nude photos
jokes.explanation: Appropriately gross character moment that establishes Razmig's creepiness.

jokes.text: You are a big woman. You have pictures? Fat nudes?
jokes.setup: Razmig talking to Liz
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate request for nude photos
jokes.explanation: Appropriately awful but predictable creepy behavior

jokes.text: I may [bleep] hate you right now, You little [bleep], but [bleep] damn it I respect you.
jokes.setup: Kenneth quitting standards to enter the contest
jokes.punchline: The profanity-laden response from standards department again
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to the ironic profanity from standards, though less impactful the second time.

jokes.text: When I went to the bathroom on the hood of your car Before I thought of this plan?
jokes.setup: Jack asking what the cruelest thing anyone has done to him is
jokes.punchline: Jenna casually mentioning she defecated on his car
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jenna's casual cruelty and lack of shame.

jokes.text: Her ruthless pursuit of self-interest. It's the heart of capitalism. And the beautiful black heart of my wife.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining what makes Avery special
jokes.punchline: Describing selfishness as her most attractive quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - romanticizing capitalism and selfishness as love.

jokes.text: I have a female condom to take out. Unless... Going once...
jokes.setup: Jenna saying she needs to excuse herself
jokes.punchline: The gross overshare followed by offering it to Jack
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna grossness with the auction format making it extra inappropriate.

jokes.text: Goo-goo, ga-ga, my fellow ah-meh-ri-cans.
jokes.setup: Tracy doing presidential impression while sleep-deprived
jokes.punchline: Baby talk mixed with presidential address
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy absurdity that connects to his deteriorating mental state.

jokes.text: 'cause I would do anything for love, But I won't do that.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining his devotion to Tracy
jokes.punchline: Using Meat Loaf lyrics to express his limits
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference that fits Kenneth's earnest character.

jokes.text: That was a real commercial for an erectile dysfunction drug.
jokes.setup: Jack complimenting a sketch about sexual performance
jokes.punchline: It wasn't actually comedy, just a real pharmaceutical ad
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about the show's desperation and the similarity between sketch comedy and real commercials.

jokes.text: That's how the banking industry learned its lesson. And now they're doing amazing, and everyone's honest.
jokes.setup: Jack defending his mentoring philosophy
jokes.punchline: His completely wrong assessment of banking post-crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack obliviousness to obvious economic reality

jokes.text: You were teaching me the whole time, And all the while lighting a fire with that hot ass.
jokes.setup: Hazel realizing Liz's mentorship strategy
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate sexual comment about her mentor
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hazel's inappropriate boundaries and attraction to Liz.

jokes.text: ♪ ahhh, do the sprinkler here we go.
jokes.setup: Contest winner Leminem performing rap
jokes.punchline: The terrible, dated dance move lyrics
jokes.explanation: Basic cringe comedy about bad performance, but not particularly clever or memorable.
